nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vernon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Vernon in Eure, France, named from the Gaulish element ver(n) ‘alder’ + the Gallo-Roman locative suffix -o (genitive -ōnis).
French: habitational name from the same place as in 1 above or from any of several other places in France with the same name and etymology.

variants.name: Varnon
meanings: English (Leicestershire and Northamptonshire): variant of   Vernon  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard de Vernon was born about 1110, in Davenham, Cheshire, England. She married Richard de Vernon about 1124, in Davenham, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard de Vernon est née environ 1110, à Davenham, Cheshire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Richard de Vernon environ 1124, à Davenham, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard de Vernon wurde um 1110 in Davenham, Cheshire, England geboren. Sie hat um 1124 in Davenham, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Richard de Vernon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard de Vernon è nata il circa 1110 nel Davenham, Cheshire, England. Lei sposato Richard de Vernon circa 1124, nel Davenham, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard de Vernon(은)는 대략 1110년 영국 체실르 대븐함에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1124년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 대븐함에서 Richard de Vernon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs Richard de Vernon родилась около 1110 г. в местности давенам, чешир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard de Vernon около 1124 г. в местности давенам, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.
